name: Corn Cakes with Sweet Pepper Relish
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Bell pepper, red; seeded & finely chopped
1 medium Bell pepper, green; seeded & finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
Corn Cakes:
1. In a large bowl, combine the flour, cornmeal, sugar, baking powder, salt, and cumin.
2. Add milk, eggs, Tabasco sauce, chopped chilies, and thawed corn to the dry ingredients. Mix well until combined.
3. Cover and refrigerate for 15 minutes.
Relish:
1. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
2. Add red and green peppers and garlic; sauté for 5 minutes.
3. Stir in lime juice, sugar, salt, and cilantro. Cook for 2 minutes, or until juices are slightly thickened.
4. Cool slightly and stir in cilantro. Refrigerate until chilled.
1. Melt a couple of slivers of butter in the pan over medium-high heat.
2. Spoon batter into the hot pan to form each cake (about 2 cakes per batch).
3. Cook for about 2 minutes on each side, or until lightly browned.
4. Continue cooking remaining batter, adding more butter as needed.
Serve corn cakes with a little relish on top. Bottled salsa can be used in place of the relish.
